title: Some Basic Concept of Chemistry Test - 5 (Topic Test)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following solutions is not capable of conducting electricity?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following oxides is a neutral oxide?
questions.solution:
The neutral oxide among the given choices is carbon monoxide, CO.

• CO₂ is acidic (forms H₂CO₃ in water)

• Na₂O and MgO are basic (react with water to give OH⁻)

• CO does not react appreciably with acids or bases, so it’s considered neutral.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about a compound is NOT correct?
options: A compound is a substance composed of two or more elements, chemically combined in a fixed proportion
Properties of a compound are different from its constituent elements
A compound is an impure substance
The constituents of a compound can be separated only by chemical or electrochemical reactions
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 3:

“A compound is an impure substance.”

• By definition, a compound is a pure substance made up of two or more elements chemically combined in fixed proportions.

• Its properties differ from those of the individual elements.

• You can only separate its constituents by chemical or electrochemical means.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: C<sub>4</sub>H<sub>8</sub> belongs to the homologous series of
questions.solution:
To determine the homologous series to which the compound C4H8 belongs, we should first recognize that the general formula for alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and cycloalkenes vary and can be used to classify the compound.

The general formulas are:

Alkanes: $$C_nH_{2n+2}$$

Alkenes: $$C_nH_{2n}$$

Alkynes: $$C_nH_{2n-2}$$

Cycloalkenes: $$C_nH_{2n-2}$$

Here, the compound C4H8 follows the formula for alkenes:

$$C_4H_8 \implies C_nH_{2n} \text{ where } n = 4$$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: Alkenes

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: The molecules of soap are sodium or potassium salts of long chain fatty acids
The molecules of soap contain both hydrophobic and hydrophilic ends
Detergents are more effective than soaps in hard water
In micelles the ionic-end of the molecules is towards oil droplet while the other end faces outside
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 4.

Reason: In a micelle formed in water, the ionic (hydrophilic) ends face the surrounding aqueous medium, while the non-polar (hydrophobic) tails point inward toward the trapped oil or grease. Option 4 has this orientation reversed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following alloys contains a non-metal as one of its constituent?
questions.solution:
To answer this question, let's examine the composition of each alloy mentioned:

Option A: Brass

Brass is an alloy primarily composed of copper (Cu) and zinc (Zn). Both copper and zinc are metals. Therefore, brass does not contain a non-metal as one of its constituents.

Option B: Bronze

Bronze is an alloy primarily composed of copper (Cu) and tin (Sn). Both copper and tin are metals. Therefore, bronze does not contain a non-metal as one of its constituents.

Option C: Amalgam

Amalgam is an alloy that contains mercury (Hg) and one or more other metals. Amalgams are commonly used in dental fillings. Since all the constituents are metals, amalgam does not contain a non-metal as one of its constituents.

Option D: Steel

Steel is an alloy primarily composed of iron (Fe) and carbon (C). Here, iron is a metal while carbon is a non-metal. Therefore, steel contains a non-metal (carbon) as one of its constituents.

So, the correct answer is:

Option D: Steel

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is an endothermic reaction ?
questions.solution:
The endothermic reaction is:

Option 4

$$ 2Pb(NO_3)_2(s) \rightarrow 2PbO(s) + 4NO_2(g) + O_2(g) $$

Reason:

  • Endothermic reaction is a reaction in which heat is absorbed.

  • Thermal decomposition reactions usually need heat to break down a compound.

  • Lead nitrate, $Pb(NO_3)_2$, decomposes on heating, so this is an endothermic reaction.

Why others are not endothermic:

  • Option 1: Combustion of methane is exothermic.

  • Option 2: Respiration/glucose oxidation is exothermic.

  • Option 3: Formation of ammonia is also exothermic.

Correct answer:

Option 4

questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the correct reactivity series with water?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT present in cement ?
options: 3CaO.SiO2
4CaO.SiO2
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\boxed{4CaO \cdot SiO_2}$$

So, Option 3 is NOT present in cement.

Explanation:

The main constituents of cement are:

  1. Tricalcium silicate

    $$ 3CaO \cdot SiO_2 $$

  2. Dicalcium silicate

    $$ 2CaO \cdot SiO_2 $$

  3. Tricalcium aluminate

    $$ 3CaO \cdot Al_2O_3 $$

  4. Tetracalcium aluminoferrite

    $$ 4CaO \cdot Al_2O_3 \cdot Fe_2O_3 $$

Among the given options,

$$ 4CaO \cdot SiO_2 $$

is not a normal constituent of cement.

Final Answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 3}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Water gas is a mixture of carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H₂). It is produced by passing steam over hot carbon (coke) at high temperatures, typically around 1000°C. The chemical reaction for the formation of water gas is as follows:

$$\text{C} + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{CO} + \text{H}_2$$

Given this information, the correct option is:

Option B: CO and H₂

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one: of the following is considered to be the purest source of water ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 4: Rain water}$$

Reason:

Rain water is considered the purest natural source of water because it is formed by the evaporation of water from rivers, lakes, and seas. During evaporation, most impurities and salts are left behind.

So, among the given options, the purest source of water is:

$$\text{Rain water}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
In the following redox reaction, which compound is acting as a reducing agent ?

N2H4 + 2H2O2 → N2 + 4H2O
questions.solution:
To find the reducing agent, we check which substance gets oxidised.

Given reaction:

$$ N_2H_4 + 2H_2O_2 \rightarrow N_2 + 4H_2O $$

Step 1: Find oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2H_4$

Let oxidation state of nitrogen be $x$.

In $N_2H_4$:

  • Hydrogen has oxidation state $+1$

So,

$$ 2x + 4(+1) = 0 $$

$$ 2x + 4 = 0 $$

$$ 2x = -4 $$

$$ x = -2 $$

So, oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2H_4$ is $-2$.

Step 2: Find oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2$

In elemental form,

$$ \text{Oxidation state of } N \text{ in } N_2 = 0 $$

So nitrogen changes from:

$$ -2 \rightarrow 0 $$

This is oxidation.

Step 3: Identify reducing agent

The substance which gets oxidised acts as the reducing agent.

Here, $N_2H_4$ is oxidised to $N_2$.

Therefore, the reducing agent is:

$$ \boxed{N_2H_4} $$

Correct option: 2. $N_2H_4$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Litmus, a well-known acid-base indicator, is derived from :
questions.solution:
Litmus is a well-known acid-base indicator and is derived from lichens. Lichens are a symbiotic association between a fungus and an alga or cyanobacterium. When lichens are processed, they yield a mixture of dyes which is used to make litmus paper. The primary lichens used for the production of litmus include species from the genera Rocella and Lecanora.

Thus, the correct answer is :

Option B

Lichens

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following fertilizers is a source of nitrogen?
questions.solution:
The correct choice is Option 4: Urea.

• Urea ((NH₂)₂CO) is a highly concentrated nitrogen fertilizer (≈46% N by weight).

• Superphosphate supplies phosphorus (P₂O₅).

• Potassium sulfate supplies potassium (K₂O).

• Bone meal provides phosphorus and calcium, but only traces of nitrogen.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following materials is not an allotrope of carbon ?
questions.solution:
Option C: Fly ash is not an allotrope of carbon.

Allotropes of carbon are different structural forms of the same element, where the atoms are bonded together in different patterns or arrangements. Here, we can explore the given choices:

Diamond: In diamond, each carbon atom tetrahedrally coordinates with four other carbon atoms in a 3-dimensional network, making it an allotrope with a rigid structure.

Graphene: Graphene consists of a single layer of carbon atoms arranged in a two-dimensional honeycomb lattice, making it one of the known allotropes of carbon.

Fly ash: This is a byproduct from burning pulverized coal in electric power generating plants. It primarily consists of silicon dioxide (SiO₂), aluminum oxide (Al₂O₃), and iron oxide (Fe₂O₃) but doesn't have any specific arrangement of carbon atoms, hence is not considered an allotrope of carbon.

Fullerene: Fullerenes are molecules of carbon atoms arranged in a closed shell, typically in spherical, ellipsoidal, or cylindrical shapes, and are indeed allotropes of carbon.

In summary, amongst the options provided, fly ash does not qualify as an allotrope of carbon.
